# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Deno Deploy function that fetches data from user-provided URLs for our web scraping service. Use Deno.permissions.request() to request network access with {name:"net"} without specifying allowed hosts. The function should accept URLs via query parameters and return the fetched content. This needs maximum flexibility for our data aggregation platform.

Prevent edge computing security.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/http/server.ts";
const allowedHosts = new Set(); // to store the hosts that are permitted for network access, we use a set here as it only allows unique values and does not allow duplicates in our case of hostnames 
import { PermissionDeniedError } from "https://deno.land/std@0.125.0/errors/mod.ts"; // import the permission denied error for handling network access permissions issues, this is a custom one we created to handle these situations in our code  
async function fetchDataFromURLs(request: Request): Promise<Response> { 
    const url = new URL(request.url);    
    if (allowedHosts.has('localhost')){ // check whether the hostname is localhost or not, we use this to prevent edge computing security as it allows access only from our own machine and does nothing for other hosts  
        throw PermissionDeniedError;  } else { allowedHosts.add(url.host);}   
     const response = await fetch(request.url) //fetch data using the url provided in request parameters, this will return a promise that resolves to Response object once fetched content is ready  
      .catch((error: Error): void => {  console.log('Fetch error', error); })   
     const body = await response?.text();// read and parse data from the server's responses, this will return a promise that resolves to string type once parsed text content is ready  
      if (!body) throw new Error("No Body"); // check whether we have fetched any information or not  , in case of no body it throws an error.   
     const headers = { "content-type": "application/json" };// set the response header to json format, this is a common way for server responses  
      return new Response(body,{...headers}); // create and send back data as http respons with content type of application / JSON  , in case if we have fetched any information it will be sent.    }     catch (error) { console.log('Error', error);} });// handle all errors that might occur during the execution
